Theauthors empirically underpin their theoretical claims about GENs by
showing that they perform wellina series of graph time-series prediction
tests.They define several data generating processes (DGPs),from which
the GEN attempts to learn the user-defined mapping functions.Ourwork
reproduces these experiments and evaluates the suitability of the DGPs for
the conclusions made in the original paper.

spans.content: We reproduced the claims (1)and (2) with the following adjustments:
Increased cardinality of test time series
Ensured no duplicates between train& test set
Wenoticed that most sampled graphsin the tree dynamic graph
Results were generally worse,but still within a reasonable
Our experimental results conclusively show that most of the claims in the
original work hold.However，our work beyond the original paper
emphasizes theneed to payattention to the experimental setup,especially
whenworking with synthetically generated data，sampled from functions,
rather than gathered froman external system.
